boundary.title: DNS supported record type writes closure

features.description: Provider-backed DNS apply supports A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, MX, SRV, and CAA records, with MX priority required.

expected_behavior: All provider writers accept A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, MX, SRV, and CAA records.
MX records require priority and propagate it to provider payloads.
Namecheap setHosts renders HostName, RecordType, Address, TTL, and MXPref fields for supported records.
Route53 renders MX records as priority-prefixed ResourceRecord values.
Cloudflare and Route53 normalize provider FQDN records to zone-relative names before reconciliation.
execution_body.goal: Open provider-backed DNS apply support for A, AAAA, CNAME, TXT, MX, SRV, and CAA records while preserving owner-scoped reconciliation and fail-closed provider writes.

features.title: DNS supported record type writes
